prediction.reasoning: Predicting stock prices over a short time frame, especially just two days, is highly uncertain and typically fluctuates within certain ranges due to market sentiment and external factors. Without detailed analysis or specific models which take into account historical volatility, news, and other economic indicators, it is common practice to assume the price will exhibit typical fluctuations around the current price point. The confidence intervals are estimates of expected price movements based on simple assumption of past data distribution rather than any specific predictive model.
